C1_conformal.finding: self-consistency is the only USEFUL nonconformity signal: selective accuracy rises 0.254->0.459 at 20% coverage (AUROC 0.614). Stated confidence (AUROC 0.580, severely overconfident ~0.93) and token logprob (AUROC 0.500, saturated) are weak. Held-out conformal coverage guarantee holds for all signals, but the useful operating region is aggressive abstention (20-30% coverage), not alpha=0.1 which answers all.

C3_abstention.finding: fail-closed abstention beats always-guessing at lambda>=0.5; but DeepSeek self-abstains only 5.5% on SimpleQA (overconfident), so the C1 self-consistency gate is what would FORCE the beneficial abstention.

honestBound: First REAL EXTERNAL (non-self-authored) validation of C1/C3, on the public human-authored SimpleQA Verified. Genuine result: the conformal mechanism is useful ONLY with self-consistency, not naive confidence. Residual gaps: single run, single grader family (need >=2 + Cohen's kappa), N=200 of 1000. canClaimAGI stays false.
